feed.title: Vishnuvardhan Sankoti
feed.subtitle: Personal blog portal showcasing tech articles, tutorials, and research notes.

entry.title: Embeddings vs. a Generative LLM for Document Matching: The Engineering Case

link.title: Embeddings vs. a Generative LLM for Document Matching: The Engineering Case

entry.summary: Resume-to-job-description matching looks like an LLM task: two documents go in, a judgment comes out. We built it without one. The system that shipped uses a 110M-parameter sentence embedding model (all-mpnet-base-v2), cosine similarity, and a deterministic scoring function — no generative model anywhere in the pipeline. This post is the engineering argument for that choice. No war stories, no...

entry.title: Deploy a Full App Stack for Nearly Free: What the Major Cloud Providers Actually Give You

link.title: Deploy a Full App Stack for Nearly Free: What the Major Cloud Providers Actually Give You

entry.summary: With AI-assisted coding making it easier than ever to build working apps, the bottleneck has shifted: not building the app, but putting it somewhere other people can actually use it. A solo developer or a small team can now go from idea to working prototype in a weekend. The problem starts on Monday, when they open a cloud provider’s pricing page. Those pages are not designed to help you spend...

entry.title: VS Code as a Full IDE: Native Tools That Replace Your External Toolchain

link.title: VS Code as a Full IDE: Native Tools That Replace Your External Toolchain

entry.summary: Most full-stack developers run three to five external tools alongside VS Code — Postman for API testing, GitHub Desktop for git, a dedicated test runner in a separate terminal, and a formatter hooked into a pre-commit script. Each context switch carries a cost: broken focus, mismatched environments, and configuration that lives outside the project repository. VS Code’s native configuration lay...

entry.title: Engineering at Scale: Notes on Architecture, Cloud, and AI

link.title: Engineering at Scale: Notes on Architecture, Cloud, and AI

entry.summary: Welcome. This is where I document the engineering decisions, architectural patterns, and research that shape the systems I build. The writing here is practical and opinionated — drawn from real-world delivery, not theory alone. About Me I am a Full-Stack Software Architect focused on designing and delivering scalable, resilient, cloud-native systems across AWS, GCP, and Azure. My work spans th...
